enonce.texte:
Match the coin or note with its correct name.

In Cameroon, we use the CFA franc (FCFA).

questions.texte: A small round metal money is called a ________.
A paper money is called a ________.
A coin with a value of 100 francs is called a ________.
questions.reponse: 100 franc coin OR one hundred franc coin

questions.texte: A note with a value of 5000 francs is called a ________.
questions.reponse: 5000 franc note OR five thousand franc note

questions.texte: What is the name of the currency used in Cameroon?

enonce.texte:
Read the price tags and answer the questions.

Price Tag Examples:

  • Bread: 500 FCFA
  • Rice (1 kg): 800 FCFA
  • Milk (1 litre): 1000 FCFA
  • Book: 2500 FCFA
  • Pen: 200 FCFA

questions.texte: How much does a book cost?
What is the price of 1 kg of rice?
Which item is more expensive: bread or milk?
questions.reponse: Milk is more expensive. Milk costs 1000 FCFA and bread costs 500 FCFA.

questions.texte: How many pens can you buy with 1000 FCFA?
If you buy a book and a pen, how much will you pay?

enonce.texte:
Read the bill and answer the questions.

📋 Supermarket Receipt

ItemQuantityPrice
Rice2 kg1600 FCFA
Oil1 litre1500 FCFA
Sugar1 kg900 FCFA
Milk2 litres2000 FCFA
Total6000 FCFA

questions.texte: List all the items on the bill.
questions.reponse: The items are: Rice, Oil, Sugar, and Milk.

questions.texte: How much does 1 litre of oil cost?
Calculate the total price of the rice.
questions.reponse: The total price of rice is 1600 FCFA (2 kg × 800 FCFA per kg).

questions.texte: Which item is the most expensive? Justify your answer.
questions.reponse: Milk is the most expensive at 2000 FCFA for 2 litres, which is 1000 FCFA per litre.

questions.texte: Verify if the total of 6000 FCFA is correct. Show your calculation.

enonce.texte:
Write the plural form of these words.

💡 Remember:

  • Add -s to most nouns (coin → coins)
  • Add -es to nouns ending in -s, -ss, -sh, -ch, -x, -z (box → boxes)

questions.texte: coin
note
bill
price
franc
bottle
box
watch

enonce.texte:
Look at the price tags in the supermarket and answer the questions.

Price Tags:

Apples
500 FCFA/kg
Bananas
300 FCFA/kg
Oranges
200 FCFA/kg
Mangoes
600 FCFA/kg

questions.texte: Which fruit is the cheapest?
questions.reponse: Oranges are the cheapest at 200 FCFA per kg.

questions.texte: How much do 3 kg of bananas cost?
Compare the price of apples and mangoes. Which is more expensive and by how much?
questions.reponse: Mangoes are more expensive. Mangoes cost 600 FCFA and apples cost 500 FCFA. The difference is 600 - 500 = 100 FCFA.

questions.texte: If you buy 2 kg of oranges and 1 kg of mangoes, how much will you pay?
questions.reponse: 2 kg of oranges = 2 × 200 = 400 FCFA. 1 kg of mangoes = 600 FCFA. Total = 400 + 600 = 1000 FCFA.

enonce.texte:
Read the shopping dialogue and complete the missing parts.

Shopping at the Market

Seller: "Good morning! Can I help you?"

Buyer: "Yes, please. How much is this rice?"

Seller: "It is ________ FCFA per kg."

Buyer: "I would like 2 kg, please."

Seller: "That will be ________ FCFA."

Buyer: "Here is 2000 FCFA."

Seller: "Thank you! Here is your change: ________ FCFA."

questions.texte: Complete the dialogue with the correct prices if 1 kg of rice costs 900 FCFA.
questions.reponse:
Complete Dialogue:

Seller: "Good morning! Can I help you?"

Buyer: "Yes, please. How much is this rice?"

Seller: "It is 900 FCFA per kg."

Buyer: "I would like 2 kg, please."

Seller: "That will be 1800 FCFA."

Buyer: "Here is 2000 FCFA."

Seller: "Thank you! Here is your change: 200 FCFA."

sousQuestions.texte: How much is the total price for 2 kg of rice?
How much change does the buyer receive from 2000 FCFA?

enonce.texte:
Calculate the total amount for the following shopping list.

Shopping List:

  • 3 kg of rice (800 FCFA/kg)
  • 2 bottles of oil (1500 FCFA/bottle)
  • 1 kg of sugar (900 FCFA)
  • 4 litres of milk (900 FCFA/litre)
  • 2 kg of apples (500 FCFA/kg)

questions.texte: Calculate the cost of each item and the total bill.
questions.reponse:
Calculations:

  • Rice: 3 kg × 800 FCFA = 2400 FCFA
  • Oil: 2 bottles × 1500 FCFA = 3000 FCFA
  • Sugar: 1 kg × 900 FCFA = 900 FCFA
  • Milk: 4 litres × 900 FCFA = 3600 FCFA
  • Apples: 2 kg × 500 FCFA = 1000 FCFA

Total: 2400 + 3000 + 900 + 3600 + 1000 = 10900 FCFA

sousQuestions.texte: If you pay with a 10,000 FCFA note and a 1,000 FCFA coin, what is your change from 11,000 FCFA?
sousQuestions.reponse: You have 11,000 FCFA. The total is 10,900 FCFA. Your change is 11,000 - 10,900 = 100 FCFA.

sousQuestions.texte: What coins could you receive as change?
sousQuestions.reponse: You could receive a 100 FCFA coin as change.
